result.summary: AfterShip exposes one public developer surface: the AfterShip Tracking HTTP API, authenticated either by API key headers or OAuth bearer token; no MCP, GraphQL, or CLI surface was confirmed.

aftership_api_key.label: AfterShip API key
aftership_api_key.setup: Go to the [API keys page](https://organization.automizely.com/api-keys) in your AfterShip organization, then create or copy an API key for your workspace. The docs point to this login/API-keys area from the Tracking API quickstart and authentication pages. Use the key in the headers described by the Tracking API docs.

aftership_oauth_token.label: AfterShip OAuth access token
aftership_oauth_token.setup: Create an AfterShip app and complete the OAuth flow described in the Tracking API OAuth docs. The flow is started from the OAuth getting-started docs; after authorization, you use the issued access token to call the API. This is the easiest path for integrations that should act on behalf of an AfterShip user/workspace.
